mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates expanding employment, boosting consumer income and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month Rank IC of PAYEMS monthly change vs 20-day forward returns is negative or below 0.05, or during non-recession periods PAYEMS increases correlate with negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at or near zero (≤0.25%), the Fed's commitment to accommodative policy is reinforced, compressing discount rates and boosting equity valuations over the next quarter.
regime_hint: monetary easing with effective lower bound
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS ≤0.25%, the proportion of positive 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 55% or the mean return is not significantly above zero (t-test p>0.10) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals broad-based economic growth, lifting corporate profits and stock prices over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where both RSAFS and INDPRO rise month-over-month, the 20-day forward S&P 500 return is negative more than 40% of the time, or the average return is indistinguishable from zero (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) above a moderate monthly pace triggers market expectations of monetary tightening, which depresses equity valuations, but only when the federal funds rate is not already near zero (normal rate regime).
failure_condition: When trigger fires and FEDFUNDS > 0.25%, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is zero or positive, or the sign of returns is positive in more than 50% of cases over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.2 percentage points over three months signals tightening labor markets, boosting consumer confidence and near-term equity prices.
failure_condition: After a UNRATE decline trigger, the 5-day forward S&P 500 return is negative more than 40% of the time or the average return is not significantly positive (p>0.10) over any 2-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strengthening consumer and residential investment, driving corporate earnings and positive stock returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In months when both HOUST and RSAFS increase month-over-month, the 20-day forward S&P 500 return is negative more than 35% of the time or the mean return is not significantly above zero (p>0.05) over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1) above 2.5% annualized signals a robust economy, leading to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Following quarters with GDP growth >2.5% annualized, the 60-day forward S&P 500 return is negative more than 30% of the time, or the average excess return over the baseline is not positive (p>0.10) over 10 years.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is near zero (≤0.25%), rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL year-over-year >2%) signals a healing economy without immediate tightening, which is positive for equities over the next quarter.
failure_condition: In zero-rate regime, when CPIAUCSL YoY >2%, the 60-day forward S&P 500 return is negative more than 40% of the time or average return is not significantly positive (p>0.05) over any 3-year window.
